issue_body: The argument is just there to support the now-deprecated `invalidate` task.

issue_title: Prefer wheels during plugin install
issue_body:
Pants has sprouted a dependency on a package that is most easily installed via a `whl` on pypi (`openssl` for `requests[security]`).

But currently the plugin installation infrastructure does not enable usage of `WheelPackage` due to a defensive avoidance of assuming that `wheel` is installed in an environment that pants is being loaded from:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/e0d5108ff75a41421a11321c73c817332f0a1c86/src/python/pants/init/plugin_resolver.py#L79-L82

As far as @kwlzn or I can tell, `wheel` should always be on the PYTHONPATH, as pants itself depends on it: it's not clear that it actually needs to be "installed" on the PATH as well.

cc @jsirois for any context he might have on that comment.

issue_title: `./pants repl` should walk through language-agnostic targets to select roots
issue_body:
In order to avoid exploding for any/all ambiguity in the graph, 4236611aa6066238b1d59380ff140b258c6df2d5 switched to using only the selected roots to determine which language to load (this allowed for node.js/scala mixed graphs, which needed to allow scala.js to depend on jar_library targets).

It seems to need another tweak though: in the case where roots are language-agnostic, we should walk through them until we locate language-specific targets.

cc @patricklaw

issue_title: Basic `Console` singleton/subsystem: stdout only
issue_body:
supports #6001.

the idea here would be to create a new `Console` class + instance that we can inject into the `Graph` at startup - which (for now) `@rules` can request and then call `Console.stdout.write('some text')` to have it go to the runners stdout.

we'd probably use a `SingletonRule` for this like so: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/init/engine_initializer.py#L278

and this could probably be a Python object to start and could just get a reference to the current value of `sys.std{out|err}` at call time to emit logging. #6005 will probably end up converting this into a Rust-backed thing.

issue_title: When sources is a string, provide a good error message
issue_body:
Currently, we don't check for str, so when `create_sources_field` calls `Files.create_fileset_with_spec(sources_rel_path, *sources)`, it splits the string into chars. If the string has no `.`s, it'll just point to a bunch of one letter files that don't exist. If it does have a `.`, eg `my_pkg.py`, then it will blow up with an error like:

```
Exception message: [Errno 21] Is a directory: u'<path-to-BUILD-file>/.'
For reference, simplified version of BUILD file:
some_target(name='whatever',
  sources='should-be-a-list.py'
)
```

issue_title: The generated coveragerc seems wrong
issue_body:
I've encountered the case where the generated coveragerc looks like this:

```
[run]
branch = True
timid = True

[report]
exclude_lines =
        def __repr__
        raise NotImplementedError

[paths]
shared =
                shared
                .pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/shared
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/.pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
error =
                error
                .pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/error
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/.pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
```

(The content is generated from this line: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/python/tasks2/pytest_run.py#L210)

The run command is: `./pants test.pytest --coverage=auto error:test -- -p no:logging`

And here's the BUILD:

```
python_tests(
    name='test',
    sources=rglobs('tests/*.py'),
    coverage='error',
    compatibility=['>=2.7','<3'],
    dependencies=[
        ':src',
        '//shared:test_libs',
        '//3rdparty/python:pyfakefs',
        '//3rdparty/python:mock'
    ]
)
```

`:src` and `//shared:test_libs` are regular `python_library`s. `:src` has the module `error`.

This leads to the generated report below:

```
18:05:50 00:03       [coverage]
                     Name                           Stmts   Miss Branch BrPart  Cover
                     ----------------------------------------------------------------
                     shared/test_libs/__init__.py       0      0      0      0   100%
                     shared/test_libs/helpers.py       38     38      8      0     0%
                     ----------------------------------------------------------------
                     TOTAL                             38     38      8      0     0%
```

You can see that it doesn't include the module `error`. If I remove `//shared:test_libs` from the BUILD target, it suddenly shows the coverage from the module `error`.

After testing, there's something strange around `[paths]`. I'm not sure.

This is Pants 1.3.0.

issue_title: Support sharing registry across multiple workers (where possible)
issue_body:
Similar to https://github.com/prometheus/client_ruby/issues/9, when using the python client library on workers that get load balanced by something like gunicorn or uwsgi, each scrape it hits only one worker since they can't share state with others.

At least uwsgi supports sharing memory: http://uwsgi-docs.readthedocs.org/en/latest/SharedArea.html
This should be used to share the registry across all workers. Maybe gunicorn supports something similar.

issue_title: Where can I find the "status" code of a response?
issue_body:
I'd love to differentiate between if a Directions API request with status "OK" or status "ZERO_RESULTS". But, the `response` does not include this status field.

This status code is inspected in [`client.py` on line 239](https://github.com/googlemaps/google-maps-services-python/blob/master/googlemaps/client.py#L237), but never stored in a response.

Can I find it somewhere else? Or is this something that could be considered to be added to the response, as returned by `googlemaps.Client`?

I'm trying to process a large dataset of Ohio addresses. 99% of them are processed perfectly. Thank you for this great resource.

There are still a substantial number of addresses that throw a RepeatedLabelError exception. They fall into a few categories

issue_body:
Hi,

I'm having trouble with the following address:
Address: 105-25 Horace Harding Expy N, Corona, NY
Corona should be a 'PlaceName'. 

Attempt at parsing:
```
Python 2.7.6 (default, Mar 22 2014, 22:59:56)
[GCC 4.8.2] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import usaddress as us
>>> s1 = '105-25 Horace Harding Expy N Corona, Queens, NY'
>>> us.tag(s1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/vagrant/dev/parsers/addressparser/env_addressparser/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/usaddress/__init__.py", line 94, in tag
    raise RepeatedLabelError(address_string, parse(address_string), label)
usaddress.RepeatedLabelError:

ERROR: Unable to tag this string because more than one area of the string has the same label

ORIGINAL STRING:  105-25 Horace Harding Expy N Corona, Queens, NY
PARSED TOKENS:    [(u'105-25', 'AddressNumber'), (u'Horace', 'StreetName'), (u'Harding', 'StreetName'), (u'Expy', 'StreetNamePostType'), (u'N', 'StreetNamePreDirectional'), (u'Corona,', 'StreetName'), (u'Queens,', 'PlaceName'), (u'NY', 'StateName')]
UNCERTAIN LABEL:  StreetName

When this error is raised, it's likely that either (1) the string is not a valid address or (2) some tokens were labeled incorrectly


```

I couldn't get the trainer to work properly with this address - it doesn't pick up the State (NY). 

```
$ cat horaceharding.csv
105-25 Horace Harding Expy N, Corona, NY

$ parserator label horaceharding.csv training/labeled.xml usaddress

1 of 1)

| Expy    | StreetNamePostType        |
| N       | StreetNamePostDirectional |
Is this correct? (y)es / (n)o / (s)kip / (f)inish tagging / (h)elp
```

Thanks in advance!

P.S. This library, documentation and support is wonderful! Thank you!

issue_title: Apache2 Reverse proxy subdirectory /kolibri
issue_body:
I found that it was difficult to deploy Kolibri using Apache2 mod_proxy module on subdirectory "/kolibri". The issues stems from the fact that django users absolute paths internally. 

Here is full Apache2 kolibri.conf configuration: 
``<VirtualHost *:80>
    ProxyPreserveHost   On
    RedirectMatch       ^/kolibri$         /kolibri/
    ProxyPass           /kolibri/          http://127.0.0.1:8009/
    ProxyPassReverse    /kolibri/          http://127.0.0.1:8009/
</VirtualHost>
``
And Kolibri Systemd unit file. 

``
[Unit]
Description=Kolibri

[Service]
Type=forking
RemainAfterExit=yes
Environment=KOLIBRI_USER=kolibri
Environment=KOLIBRI_HOME=/opt/kolibri
Environment=KOLIBRI_HTTP_PORT=8009
Environment=ALLOWED_HOSTS=localhost
Environment=USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST=True

ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/kolibri start
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/kolibri stop

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
``
And the downstream bug: https://github.com/iiab/iiab/issues/913

issue_body:
## Summary

A long-needed change. Thanks to @laurenlichtman for bringing it up.

**Current order of the content recommendation subsections:**

1. Most popular
2. Next steps
3. Resume
4. Overview (to be renamed)

**Proposed reordering:**

1. Resume
2. Next steps
3. Most popular
4. Overview (to be renamed)

**Justification:**
Presenting "Resume" followed by "Next steps" is more in line with supporting a personalized learning experience. The Learner would see content that is most related to what they were most recently engaging with first, without needing to scroll or click through a labyrinth of topics.

Please write comments providing further justifications for either ordering, as there is not consensus in the team for the proposed reordering.

## System information

*Please specify the Kolibri version you were using and your operating system!*

 - Version: Kolibri 0.5.0beta1.dev33
 - Operating system: MacOS Sierra
 - Browser: Chrome

issue_title: re-ordering in emit_xml()
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm trying to build a variable to pass to the REST API. What I've found is that when the variable is is passed to XMLDictNode and emit_xml is called, things get re-ordered and this causes the rest api to reject the request. If I create the config var by parsing an XML string, the reordering does not happen. I even used the output from print to configure the variable, but the reordering still happens when the variable is set directly. I've included a small test program that exhibits this behaviour. Any idea why this happens or how to avoid it?

```
#!/usr/bin/env python
"""test

"""

import sys
import email
import getpass
import pprint

import requests
import jxmlease


# Create a jxmlease parser with desired defaults.
parser = jxmlease.Parser()

def main():
    """The main loop.
    """
    # parsing XML works
    root = jxmlease.parse("""<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<configuration>
    <logical-systems>
       <name>R30</name>
       <firewall>
          <policer>
             <name>30M</name>
             <if-exceeding>
                 <bandwidth-limit>30m</bandwidth-limit>
                    <burst-size-limit>30M</burst-size-limit>

             <then>
                <discard/>
             </then>
          </policer>
       </firewall>
    </logical-systems>
</configuration>""")
    print('----root----')

    config_node = jxmlease.XMLDictNode(root)

    print('---config_node---')
    config_node.prettyprint()
    print('---config_node.xml---')
    print config_node.emit_xml()

    print('****    ****')

    # creating confg directly results in re-ordered xml output

    config_node = jxmlease.XMLDictNode(config)

    print('---config_node.xml---')
    print config_node.emit_xml()
    print('----    ----')
    return config_node

if __name__ == "__main__":
  sys.exit(main())

issue_title: search parameter validation
issue_body:
The PyMISP [search functionality](https://github.com/MISP/PyMISP/blob/e39d014ddca875518a810bc0c2de7be845b6c99b/pymisp/api.py#L893) does not validate the parameter keys that are passed. If you misspell a parameter (`attribute_type` vs. `type_attribute`), PyMISP will simply ignore it. This is dangerous insofar as you cannot determine on first glance that something went wrong, since PyMISP returns valid JSON.

My simple-minded approach would be to pop each parameter from `**kwargs` instead of getting them and alerting the user if arguments from the dict are not popped yet (`Unused parameter: attribute_type`). Perhaps you have a better idea.

issue_title: Django 1.7 compatibilty
issue_body:
#51 was my naive attempt to use django-pyodbc with 1.7b1, it seems to be more complicated...

This is what I've found so far:
1. A `ProgrammingError: Attempt to use a closed cursor` is raised on select queries.[commit comment](https://github.com/django/django/commit/0837eacc4e1fa7916e48135e8ba43f54a7a64997#commitcomment-6053889)
   If I replace `cursor.close()` with `pass` on django/db/models/sql/compiler.sql:820, selecting stuff in the shell works again. But it obviously needs to be fixed at `django-pyodbc`s side.
2. The use of `TEST_*` prefixes in the `DATABASES` setting is deprecated, but `django-pyodbc` assumes the keys exist. This makes test database creation fail.
3. Schema editors. Django 1.7 ships with migrations, so the `BaseDatabaseWrapper` defines a method `schema_editor` which raises `NotImplementedError`.
   
   ```
   ...
   File "/home/jieter/.virtualenvs/pyodbc-test/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/backends/__init__.py", line 546, in schema_editor
   raise NotImplementedError('subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a schema_editor() method')
   NotImplementedError: subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a schema_editor() method
   ```
   
   If I add a method returning a simple SchemaEditor like this:
   
   ``` python
   def schema_editor(self, *args, **kwargs):
       "Returns a new instance of this backend's SchemaEditor"
       return DatabaseSchemaEditor(self, *args, **kwargs)
   ```
   
   (together with a `DatabaseSchemaEditor` extending `BaseDatabaseSchemaEditor`), I get a `TransactionManagementError` when `./manage.py runserver`:
   
   ```
   ...
   File "/home/jieter/.virtualenvs/pyodbc-test/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/migrations/loader.py", line 148, in build_graph
     self.applied_migrations = recorder.applied_migrations()
   File "/home/jieter/.virtualenvs/pyodbc-test/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/migrations/recorder.py", line 48, in applied_migrations
     self.ensure_schema()
   File "/home/jieter/.virtualenvs/pyodbc-test/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/migrations/recorder.py", line 41, in ensure_schema
     with self.connection.schema_editor() as editor:
   File "/home/jieter/.virtualenvs/pyodbc-test/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/backends/schema.py", line 74, in __enter__
     atomic(self.connection.alias, self.connection.features.can_rollback_ddl).__enter__()
   File "/home/jieter/.virtualenvs/pyodbc-test/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/django/db/transaction.py", line 274, in __enter__
     "The outermost 'atomic' block cannot use "
   django.db.transaction.TransactionManagementError: The outermost 'atomic' block cannot use savepoint = False when autocommit is off.
   ```

idea's?

issue_title: Duplicate peer definition may be non-fatal
issue_body:
##### ISSUE TYPE
 - Feature Idea

##### OS
Debian Linux

##### VERSION
```
ExaBGP : 4.0.2-1c737d99
Python : 3.6.4 (default, Dec 19 2017, 14:09:48)  [GCC 7.2.0]
Uname  : Linux zoro 4.14.0-1-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 4.14.2-1 (2017-11-30) x86_64
Root   :
```

##### CONFIGURATION
```
neighbor 80.81.195.174 {
 ...
}
neighbor 80.81.195.174 {
 ...
}
```

##### SUMMARY

Currently, when a neighbor is defined twice, we get a "duplicate peer definition" error. However, when ExaBGP is not using `listen`, there should be no problem in connecting to a peer twice. This could be done with a different `local-address` for each connection.

Here is a proposition to fix that:

 - accept a non-IP name for `neighbor`
 - `peer-address` defaults to name if this is an IP
 - ensure `peer-address` is present (either through name or directly)
 - modify unicity check to use name and not `peer-address`

Am I missing something?

Run container:

```

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

import tarfile

import docker

    line = "-" * 79
    line += "\n"

        fd.write(data)

        data = compose_data()
        write_data(FNAME, data)
        make_tar(TAR_NAME, "./" + FNAME)

        client = docker.from_env(version="auto")
        container = client.containers.get(CONTAINER_NAME)

        with open(TAR_NAME, 'rb') as fd:

    # Cleanup

## Some thoughts

Digging into this I've found that it's actually a **requests** known issue, [raised several times already](https://github.com/requests/requests/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue%20is%3Aclosed%20broken%20pipe%20). From [this discussion](https://github.com/requests/requests/issues/2422) it seems that it will not get fixed any time soon.

Meantime as a workaround it's possible to catch `requests.exceptions.ConnnectionError` in `res = self._put(url, params=params, data=data)` call here:

```python
    @utils.check_resource('container')
    @utils.minimum_version('1.20')
    def put_archive(self, container, path, data):
        """
        Insert a file or folder in an existing container using a tar archive as
        source.

        Args:
            container (str): The container where the file(s) will be extracted
            path (str): Path inside the container where the file(s) will be
                extracted. Must exist.
            data (bytes): tar data to be extracted

        Returns:
            (bool): True if the call succeeds.

        Raises:
            :py:class:`docker.errors.APIError`
                If the server returns an error.
        """
        params = {'path': path}
        url = self._url('/containers/{0}/archive', container)
        res = self._put(url, params=params, data=data)
        self._raise_for_status(res)
        return res.status_code == 200

```

and try to compose `docker.errors.NotFound` similarly as it is done in `docker.errors.create_api_error_from_http_exception`. 

I can try to compose a PR for this.

issue_title: Error: name X is already in use by container Y
issue_body:
While using docker-py in two cron jobs, `create_container()` in one of them produced the error message:

    APIError: 409 Client Error: Conflict ("{"message":"Conflict. The name \"/temp_cont\" is already in use by container <HASH>. You have to remove (or rename) that container to be able to reuse that name."}") 

Obviously, this was caused by using the same name for the temporary container in both jobs. However, the container doesn't exist anymore and I still get the error when running only one of the jobs manually. In fact, I even tried using a different name. So I'm not trying to name it "temp_cont" anymore, but it thinks I am. It seems to be caching the previous command somewhere.

I'm only seeing this issue using docker-py, and not using `docker create` or `docker run` from the command line. `docker ps --all` doesn't show a container with that hash, and it's not in `/var/lib/docker/containers`. 

Also see the related [issue](https://github.com/docker/docker/issues/23371) in the docker repo.

issue_title: Installing on macOS
issue_body:
The installation instructions recommend to use brew to build (as Unix dependencies such as *cmake* and *swig* are required).

The installation may fail if a more recent python 2.x version has been installed with brew, even if a virtual env is used, because the system mixes libraries from the Apple installation (from `/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Python`) and from the brew installation (from `/usr/local/Cellar/python/2.7.*/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Python`).

When the simplest test program is started, the Python VM fails with the ciphered error message `Fatal Python error: PyThreadState_Get: no current thread`

You need to (re)build the pc-ble-driver library with the system Python VM.

brew does not install boost libraries with universal builds. If you do not want to mess your system with an old boost library and that you run a 64 bit host - which should be most of the macOS installations nowadays - install boost normally with brew, then edit `apple.cmake` file and remove 32 bit support:

    set(CMAKE_OSX_ARCHITECTURES "x86_64")

It would be nice to see all those tools, instructions, everything updated. 
GCC 4.9 (to build nRF52 SDK) is outdated, 32-bit PC installations are outdated, Python SWIG-based wrappers are outdated, Boost 1.55 is nearly 4 years old, Python 2.x is outdated etc, etc. What a pity nRF52 is a new product, but relies on such outdated tools.

issue_title: Access response headers in "get_iterator_next_request_kwargs"
issue_body:
Some API's (Githubs for instance) pass info required to define the "next" endpoint to be fetched in response headers.
Given this, response headers should be available in ```get_iterator_next_request_kwargs```.
For this to work, it's needed to pass the raw response to the ```TapiocaClient``` generated after a request and change the method definition of ```get_iterator_next_request_kwargs```.

Here are some of the possible changes that can be made:
1. Pass ```response``` as a kwarg
````
python
def get_iterator_next_request_kwargs(self, iterator_request_kwargs, response_data, response=None):
    ...
```

2. remove ```response_data``` and pass only ```response```
```python
def get_iterator_next_request_kwargs(self, iterator_request_kwargs, response):
    ...
```

This discussion was initiated by @gileno

issue_title: Implement binary JSON auto-conversion
issue_body:
The WAMP spec describes a method to encode binary strings in JSON by prepending the base64 encoding of the binary string with `\0`. See [here](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-oberstet-hybi-tavendo-wamp-02#page-131).

This isn't implemented. It would need to hook into the string processing with the JSON serializer.

More so, as we target PyPy anyway, we should probably aim for writing our own, bundled JSON thing:

* implements transparent binary bidirectional conversion
* pure Python, optimized for PyPy

We should have more unit tests regarding cross-serializer transparency. I think this is quite important, since it's one of the selling points of WAMP.

issue_title: to_number does not correctly handle floating point numbers without dots
issue_body:
The [JMESPath specification of the to_number function](http://jmespath.readthedocs.io/en/latest/specification.html#to-number) states that everything matching the `json-number` production must be supported. However, this implementation will handle floating point numbers incorrectly if they do not contain a dot.

Please consider the following example
```json
{"x": "1e+21"}
```

When searched with the JMESPath
```
x | to_number(@)
```

this implementation will return None since the number is not recognized as such. The parser will only try parsing a floating point number if it contains a dot, as can be seen [in the code](https://github.com/jmespath/jmespath.py/blob/develop/jmespath/functions.py#L205)

Therefore, the following (incorrect) behavior can be observed
```python
$ python
Python 2.7.12 (default, Nov 19 2016, 06:48:10) 
[GCC 5.4.0 20160609] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import jmespath
>>> data = {"x": "1e+21"}
>>> jmespath.search("x | to_number(@)", data)
>>> 
```

Please note the None return value which is not printed by the REPL.

The JSON standard allows such numbers, as per http://www.json.org/ where the grammar allows a special case of number `number := int exp` where `exp := e digits` and `e := 'e+'`.

  `  conda install gdal`

but I can't load it. If I run Python

    ---> 17                 _mod = imp.load_module('_gdal', fp, pathname, description)
         18             finally:

        240                 return load_dynamic(name, filename, opened_file)
        241         else:
    --> 242             return load_dynamic(name, filename, file)
        243     elif type_ == PKG_DIRECTORY:
        244         return load_package(name, filename)
    
    /home/auerilas/anaconda3/lib/python3.5/imp.py in load_dynamic(name, path, file)

        341             name=name, loader=loader, origin=path)
    --> 342         return _load(spec)
        343
        344 else:
    
    ImportError: libnetcdf.so.7: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
```

I run into the same error if I try to run gdal from the command line

```
    gdalwarp
    gdalwarp: error while loading shared libraries: libnetcdf.so.7: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
```

However, it looks as though libnetcdf is installed:

 ```
   ls -R | grep libnetcdf
    libnetcdf-4.4.1-0.json
    libnetcdf.a
    libnetcdf.la
    libnetcdf.settings
    libnetcdf.so
    libnetcdf.so.11
    libnetcdf.so.11.0.3
    libnetcdf-4.4.1-0
    libnetcdf-4.4.1-0.tar.bz2
    ./anaconda3/pkgs/libnetcdf-4.4.1-0:

    ./anaconda3/pkgs/libnetcdf-4.4.1-0/include:
    ./anaconda3/pkgs/libnetcdf-4.4.1-0/info:
    ./anaconda3/pkgs/libnetcdf-4.4.1-0/lib:
    libnetcdf.a
    libnetcdf.la
    libnetcdf.settings
    libnetcdf.so
    libnetcdf.so.11
    libnetcdf.so.11.0.3
    ./anaconda3/pkgs/libnetcdf-4.4.1-0/lib/pkgconfig:

```
Does anyone have any ideas? I've tried compiling gdal from source and that doesn't work, I run into an error about gdalinfo missing. However, gdal config works

```
    gdal-config --version
    2.0.0

```
and I've also tried running 

`    sudo apt-get install libgdal1-dev`

I hacked together a solution by following the broken links. When I installed gdal, I had libnetcdf.so.11, libkea.so.1.4.6, and libhdf5_cpp.so.12. But gdal was looking for libnetcdf.so.7, libkea.so.1.4.5, and libhdf5_cpp.10. I made the following symlinks:

```
    cd ~/anaconda3/lib
    ln -s libnetcdf.so.11 libnetcdf.so.7
    ln -s libkea.so.1.4.6 libkea.so.1.4.5
    ln -s libhdf5_cpp.12 libhdf5_cpp.10
```

and it works fine. Something was broken in the install where gdal was attempting to find the libraries by specific version rather than by the generic libnetcdf.so. If gdal uses the generic, non-versioned files, those act as symbolic links to the current version. It may just be that gdal needs to be updated to the current version on conda?

issue_title: user config and multiple miniconda/anaconda installations
issue_body:
If one were to centrally administer several conda installs, and users were then to create their own environments, I think there may be issues. I'm thinking of something along the lines of a production miniconda install, and a development miniconda install. I'd prefer just one miniconda install where I maintain separate environments for production vs. development, but there are some packages which can only live in the root environment - namely conda, conda-env and I think conda-build? I'm concerned about issues like https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/3104 where updating conda can leave conda inoperable. I'm thinking the production install will not have conda-build in it. 

As I look at the .condarc and .conda directory that get created for a user of the central install, I can see someone creating an environment based on the production miniconda, which would include links to packages in the production miniconda, but then they may activate this environment from the development miniconda, with unforseen side effects.

I suspect this is an unusual use case, low priority issue, but thought it would be worth noting. I imagine the same issue would arise for a user that is playing two miniconda installs, or a miniconda and a anaconda install.

issue_title: install.extract fails with conda installed on NFS, when package is in use
issue_body:
Trying to create a new environment, using an explicit package listing and a conda installed on a shared NFS mount.

From the error message:

```
Current conda install:

               platform : linux-64
          conda version : 4.2.12
       conda is private : False
      conda-env version : 4.2.12
    conda-build version : not installed
         python version : 3.5.2.final.0
       requests version : 2.11.1
       root environment : .../miniconda3  (writable)
    default environment : .../miniconda3
       envs directories : .../miniconda3/envs
          package cache : .../miniconda3/pkgs
           channel URLs : https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/linux-64
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/noarch
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/linux-64
                          https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/noarch
            config file : None
           offline mode : False

...

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/.../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/exceptions.py", line 479, in conda_exception_handler
        return_value = func(*args, **kwargs)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 145, in _main
        exit_code = args.func(args, p)
      File "../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main_create.py", line 68, in execute
        install(args, parser, 'create')
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/install.py", line 191, in install
        explicit(specs, prefix, verbose=not context.quiet, index_args=index_args)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/misc.py", line 187, in explicit
        execute_actions(actions, index=index, verbose=verbose)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/plan.py", line 643, in execute_actions
        inst.execute_instructions(plan, index, verbose)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/instructions.py", line 134, in execute_instructions
        cmd(state, arg)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/instructions.py", line 58, in EXTRACT_CMD
        extract(arg)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/install.py", line 807, in extract
        exp_backoff_fn(os.rename, temp_path, path)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/common/disk.py", line 148, in exp_backoff_fn
        return fn(*args, **kwargs)
    FileExistsError: [Errno 17] File exists: '.../miniconda3/pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3.tmp' -> '.../miniconda3/pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3'

```

The main issue AFAICT is that some process has a file under the pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3 folder open (probably a .so). When install.extract() tries to rm_rf() the target package path, NFS v3 uses "silly rename" to act like the open file got deleted, but it leaves a .nfs....... file in place of the original file. So the rm_rf of the target package path silently fails in install.extract(), then the os.rename loudly fails.

The .nfs...... files will not be removed until the process that has them open exits. In the case of a daemon process, this could be a while.

A "silly rename" like work-around is:
```
--- install.py	2017-01-18 14:26:31.394302133 +0000
+++ install.py.new	2017-01-18 15:23:46.090889706 +0000
@@ -35,6 +35,7 @@
 import sys
 import tarfile
 import traceback
+import datetime
 from collections import namedtuple
 from enum import Enum
 from itertools import chain
@@ -805,6 +806,12 @@
         with tarfile.open(fname) as t:
             t.extractall(path=temp_path)
         rm_rf(path)
+        # In case the rm_rf does not fully remove everything (e.g. permission
+        # denied, open files on NFS mount, etc.), move it out of the way
+        if os.path.exists(path):
+            timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().isoformat().replace(':', '_')
+            backup_path = path + '.' + timestamp
+            exp_backoff_fn(os.rename, path, backup_path)
         exp_backoff_fn(os.rename, temp_path, path)
         if sys.platform.startswith('linux') and os.getuid() == 0:
             # When extracting as root, tarfile will by restore ownership
```

But this will likely require manual cleanup.

issue_title: customized `cd` with output breaks sourced activate/deactivate
issue_body:
In bash on osx I have a customized `cd` like:
```
function cd() {
    builtin cd "$@" || return;

    # Need -w with pipe for 78 sized screen
    ls -CFh -w $COLUMNS | head -n 25;
}
```

Since activate and deactivate are sourced, they will be in scope for this cd, causing the following errors:

```
$ source activate snowflakes
discarding /Users/$USER/anaconda/envs/snowflakes/bin from PATH
prepending /Users/$USER/anaconda/envs/snowflakes/bin to PATH
ls: 115: No such file or directory

$ source deactivate 
ls: 115: No such file or directory
discarding /Users/$USER/anaconda/envs/snowflakes/bin from PATH
```

The relevant lines are:

```
get_dirname() {
    echo "$(cd "$(dirname "$1")" && pwd)"
}
...
    _DIR="$( cd -P "$(dirname $_ABS_SCRIPT_LOCATION)" && pwd )"
```

This is low priority, but should also be easy to fix, perhaps just using `builtin cd` instead.
Manually replacing the `builtin` resolved the issue for me.

Thanks

issue_title: Conda checks for newer versions in channels in current context instead of only base channels
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior My base environment uses only the default channels. When I attempted to create a new environment using the conda-forge channel, conda found a newer conda version in conda-forge and warned about it. issue_title: conda clean -p seems to break hard linking of new installs
issue_body:
@asmeurer 
Following on from conda/conda#68:

On Windows 8 (and probably equally on all other windows versions), `conda clean -p` removes all extracted packaged from the `pkgs` directory even if they are hard linked in some environments.

While all existing environments will continue to function, creation of a new environment with the same packages causes conda to extract the packages again and  to hard link to the newly extracted files. This massively increases disk usage and results in the opposite of what a user running `conda clean` was attempting to achieve.

The problem lies in `main_clean.py` and the fact that on Windows  `lstat(file).st_nlink` always returns 0, even if `file` is hard linked. (This seems to have been fixed from python 3.2 onwards: https://bugs.python.org/issue10027)

As a stop gap measure `conda clean -p` should be prevented from being run on windows until a better solution can be found.

```
C:\>conda list
# packages in environment at C:\Anaconda:
#
conda                     3.10.1                   py27_0
conda-env                 2.1.4                    py27_0
menuinst                  1.0.4                    py27_0
psutil                    2.2.1                    py27_0
pycosat                   0.6.1                    py27_0
python                    2.7.9                         1
pyyaml                    3.11                     py27_0
requests                  2.6.0                    py27_0
```

issue_title: Limit number of compounds for SVs
issue_body:
The number of compounds retrieved grows to big if there are many variants in a gene that is requested or if the SV spanns many genes.

**Action**
Add a sort and limit to the query when getting compounds for svs

**Solution**
```python

issue_title: TLAST not required on every axi_stream_slave transaction
issue_body:
Currently, every transaction of the axi_stream_slave requires that TLAST be set to '1', or else a check in the axi_stream_slave entity will fail, causing a failure of the VUnit test. However, this is TLAST is not a requirement of the AXIS protocol and should only come on the last data beat of a packetized data stream.

As a workaround, I commented out the check in axi_stream_slave, as well as added a pop_axi_stream procedure (similar to the push_axi_stream procedure) that returns TLAST so that I can check/act on it myself.

issue_title: axis tuser
issue_body:
Hi

I tried to add TUSER to the axi stream VC. I'm currently stuck with the optional character of the signal.
How would you implement optional parameters in both stream_master_pkg and axi_stream_pkg?

The stream_master_pkg does not know the length of tuser, so having a push_stream function without it fails because it cannot push the correct vector to the message. Or should I modify the protocol if there is no tuser data (i.e. somethimes push 3 values, sometimes 2)?
So I just left stream_master_pkg as is (tuser is axi specific anyway) and made the axi_stream_master drive tuser=zero if the message was not axi specific.

However, on the slave side, this distinction does not work as there is no pop_axi_stream_msg. There is no client function to receive the tuser signal (no pop_axi_stream).
What would be the best way to implement this?

Thanks and best regards,
emanuel

issue_title: Curator not respecting prefix
issue_body:
Details:

```
➜  ~ curator --version
curator, version 3.5.0
```

I have the following indices in my local environment:

```
➜  ~ curl localhost:9200/_cat/indices
yellow open index-2016.09.10   5 1 0 0   575b   575b
yellow open index-2016.09.39   5 1 0 0   575b   575b
yellow open index2016.09.39    5 1 0 0   575b   575b
green  open .marvel-2016.10.10 1 0 0 0 16.1kb 16.1kb
```

When using a daily time units, the prefix is not being respected. It's still trying to extract the timestamp from indices that the prefix is not match:

```
➜  ~ curator show indices --older-than 2 --time-unit days --timestring '%Y.%m.%d' --prefix 'sdafksadlfsdf-'
2016-10-10 09:22:26,499 INFO      Job starting: show indices
2016-10-10 09:22:26,509 ERROR     Could not extract a timestamp matching 2016.09.39 from timestring %Y.%m.%d
2016-10-10 09:22:26,509 ERROR     Could not extract a timestamp matching 2016.09.39 from timestring %Y.%m.%d
2016-10-10 09:22:26,509 WARNING   No indices matched provided args: {'regex': None, 'index': (), 'suffix': None, 'newer_than': None, 'closed_only': False, 'prefix': u'sdafksadlfsdf-', 'time_unit': 'days', 'timestring': u'%Y.%m.%d', 'exclude': (), 'older_than': 2, 'all_indices': False}
No indices matched provided args: {'regex': None, 'index': (), 'suffix': None, 'newer_than': None, 'closed_only': False, 'prefix': u'sdafksadlfsdf-', 'time_unit': 'days', 'timestring': u'%Y.%m.%d', 'exclude': (), 'older_than': 2, 'all_indices': False}
```

Note that it's trying to extract the timestamp for *index-2016.09.39* and *index2016.09.39*. Given that we know the prefix at this point, i would expect curator to check the prefix before parsing the date. If the prefix is match, then i think that it's fine to raise the ERROR because the weekly indices cannot be extracted by a daily pattern.

@untergeek what do you think? Is this something that we can address?

Thanks!

issue_title: update option "doc_as_upsert" needs text string "true" instead of boolean "True"
issue_body:
Hi

Tried using "doc_as_upsert" in my updates. If the body of my request had a boolean value (ie "'doc_as_upsert': True") it didn't create new docs (it updated the existing ones, though). I needed to use 'true' in a text string (ie "'doc_as_upsert' : 'true'"), and then it created new documents. 

That means this call doesn't create new documents:

```
es.update(index="my_index", doc_type="my_doc_type", id=my_id, body={"doc":my_dict_update,'doc_as_upsert':True})
```

But this call does:

```
es.update(index="my_index", doc_type="my_doc_type", id=my_id, body={"doc":my_dict_update,'doc_as_upsert':'true'})
```

Maybe it's not a bug and it should work like that, as elasticsearch is expecting the string and not the boolean (maybe that should change in elasticsearch?). In that case, it would be a good idea to add a note to the documentation (I can try to help with that).

I'm using elasticsearch-py version 2.4 and elasticsearch version 2.3.4.

Thanks again!

issue_title: Compatibility with host parsing when sniffing host in elasticsearch ver 1.x
issue_body:
Host sniffing use "/_nodes/_all/clear" to get hosts information,
where address returned in elasticsearch ver 1.x in format `inet\[/IP:PORT\]` like
```
"transport_address" : "inet[/192.168.0.2:9300]"
```
while in ver 2.x in format `[HOSTNAME/]IP:PORT` like
```
"transport_address" : "192.168.0.2:9300"
```

Such case cause incompatibility with parsing host info using client ver 2.x in elasticsearch 1.x, which obstructs upgrading from 1.x to 2.x in some respects.

issue_title: Add multiple NICs per VM support to VMSS create
[Batch] Command returns parse error for resource not found
issue_body:
```
$ az batch application show -g dfgdfg -n dfgfhg --application-id dffdggfh 
usage: az batch application show [-h] [--output {json,tsv,table,jsonc}]
                                 [--verbose] [--debug] [--query JMESPATH]
                                 --resource-group RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME
                                 --application-id APPLICATION_ID --name
                                 ACCOUNT_NAME
az batch application show: error: Azure Error: ResourceGroupNotFound
Message: Resource group 'dfgdfg' could not be found.
```

Specifying a resource group that isn't found is not a parsing error.
A resource exists check should not happen here.

```
def application_enabled(namespace):
    """Validates account has auto-storage enabled"""
    from azure.mgmt.batch import BatchManagementClient
    from azure.cli.core.commands.client_factory import get_mgmt_service_client

    client = get_mgmt_service_client(BatchManagementClient)
    acc = client.batch_account.get(namespace.resource_group_name, namespace.account_name)
    if not acc:
        raise ValueError("Batch account '{}' not found.".format(namespace.account_name))
    if not acc.auto_storage or not acc.auto_storage.storage_account_id:  # pylint: disable=no-member
        raise ValueError("Batch account '{}' needs auto-storage enabled.".
                         format(namespace.account_name))
```
For `batch application` commands, there's a validator called `application_enabled` causing a parse error.

This is inconsistent with the rest of the CLI.

issue_title: azure login through interactive mode: cant recognizer user identifier or password
issue_body:
### Description
Basically, I already solved my problem, and as I did not find any information to such kind of issues, in case if somebody else will face that too, I decided to describe it here. 

So, I was having an issue during interactive login using Azure CLI 1.0 from my Linux VM. And the key of interactive login is that you need to follow the link and input the code from the command line. As I was connected to my VM only through Putty from Windows, I was not be able to go to that link and specify the code, thats whyI was using my browser on Windows. And the thing is when you trying to do that, it shows an error in the browser like this: 
> Can not recognizer user identifier or password
And it solves just installing the CLI tool on Windows and following all the steps for login. 
Hope somebody will find this helpful.

P.S.: please fix problems with urllib3 on CLI 2.0 login 
P.S.S.: put it to the appropriate place as FAQ, whatever. Or delete it if there are already such info provided.

I wanted to create an auth file from an existing SPN so I searched the source for sdk-auth and found it was also on account show.

```
PS C:\> az account show -h

Command
    az account show: Get the details of a subscription.
        If the subscription isn't specified, shows the details of the default subscription.

Arguments
    --sdk-auth       : Output result in compatible with Azure SDK auth file.
    --subscription -s: Name or ID of subscription.

Global Arguments
    --debug          : Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
    --help -h        : Show this help message and exit.

But when I tried to use it I get an error:

SDK Auth file is only applicable on service principals

I have to admit I thought it would be odd on the account as well. But the --sdk-auth is not available on ad sp show as I expected.

Looks like that options was registered by mistake on the account.

issue_title: OS Disk name for vm create is not stable -> command is not idempotent
issue_body:
From the vm_create function:

    ...
    os_disk_name = os_disk_name or 'osdisk_{}'.format(random_string(10))
    ....

This means that if I invoke the command twice with the same parameters (with os disk name omitted), I will (with a very high probability :)) end up with two disks, one of which is orphaned. This goes against the goal of having idempotent commands.

issue_title: --encrypt-format-all UUID issue when run for DATA disks only
issue_body:
The --encrypt-format-all parameter will also cause the UUIDs to be changed post encryption, therefore adding the disks into the /etc/fstab with UUIDs before encryption will most likely result in a "no boot" situation for that VM due to wrong UUID. Removing that parameter allows the system keeping the same UUIDs. Please have this tested on your own end.

issue_title: Feature Request: Progress Tracking Performant for Large Folders
issue_body:
Currently progress tracking is part of "verbose" output which inherently implies that it may not be particularly performant. This is true for very large folders, as the way progress tracking is done ultimately results in thread locking and out of sync transfer reporting status.

This feature request is to rework progress tracking to be separate from verbose output and handle large folders of very small files efficiently.

Possible solutions are:
1. Update progress less frequently (once every N files completing)
2. Track progress in a separate thread, if possible.
3. Track progress as a raw number of bytes instead of on an individual file basis (unsure if this resolves the locking issue).

issue_title: IK solution is problematic after setting the orientation for the robot
issue_body:
I found that setting the orientation for the robot can cause problems for IK. Unless the orientation is set to [0, 0, 0, 1], the IK solution could be wrong. With larger deviation from [0, 0, 0, 1], the IK solution would be more problematic.

The problem can be demonstrated by the code below (please make sure you have the `kuka_iiwa/model.urdf` from the data folder):

`
    import argparse

    import numpy as np

    import pybullet


    def parse_args():
        """Parse arguments.

        Returns:
            args: The parsed arguments.
        """
        parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()

        parser.add_argument(
                '--debug',
                dest='debug',
                type=int,
                default=0,
                help='Set to 1 to see the working version.')

        args = parser.parse_args()

        return args


    def main():
        args = parse_args()

        client_uid = pybullet.connect(pybullet.GUI)
        pybullet.resetSimulation()
        pybullet.setGravity(0,0,0)
        pybullet.setRealTimeSimulation(0)
        pybullet.setTimeStep(1e-3)

        LIMB_URDF = ('kuka_iiwa/model.urdf')

        if args.debug:
            base_quat=[0, 0, 0, 1]
        else:
            base_quat=[0, 0, 0.707, 0.707]

        sawyer_uid = pybullet.loadURDF(
                LIMB_URDF,
                basePosition=[0, 0, 0],
                baseOrientation=base_quat,
                useFixedBase=True,
                )
        ee_link_ind = 6

        num_joints = pybullet.getNumJoints(sawyer_uid)
        for joint_ind in range(num_joints):
            pybullet.resetJointState(sawyer_uid, joint_ind, 0)

        target_pos = [0, 0, 0.7]

        t = 0
        while(1):
            joint_positions = pybullet.calculateInverseKinematics(
                    bodyUniqueId=sawyer_uid,
                    endEffectorLinkIndex=ee_link_ind,
                    targetPosition=target_pos,
                    )

            for joint_ind, joint_position in enumerate(joint_positions):
                pybullet.resetJointState(
                        bodyUniqueId=sawyer_uid,
                        jointIndex=joint_ind,
                        targetValue=joint_position)

            if t == 1000:
                pos, quat, _, _, _, _ = pybullet.getLinkState(
                        sawyer_uid,
                        ee_link_ind)

                print(pos, quat)

            pybullet.stepSimulation()
            t += 1


    if __name__ == '__main__':
        main()
`

If you set the orientation to [0, 0, 0, 1] by running `python experiments/debug_ik_2.py --debug 1`, the result will make sense. 

If you set the orientation to [0, 0, 0.707, 0.707] by running `python experiments/debug_ik_2.py --debug 0`, the result will make sense.

issue_title: Build issue with libindy/include/indy_types.h
issue_body:
Hi all,

I recently started digging into indy, and was trying to compile the code and ran into this issue.

In the recent commit of  `libindy/include/indy_types.h`

https://github.com/hyperledger/indy-sdk/commit/4cafeda266a087836425a69b3f2951be6efde697#diff-6ab3e784eca5e2160eaad8ae4d050b30

These two lines were added
```
typedef long long     indy_i64_t;
typedef unsigned long long     indy_u64_t;
```
And their names collide with the existing member variables
```
typedef int64_t       indy_i64_t;
typedef uint64_t      indy_u64_t;
```

A little background, I was compiling the code with the nodeJS wrapper
https://github.com/Picolab/indy-sdk/tree/master/wrappers/nodejs

Build Log (sorry that it's long)
```
root@ubuntudev-vm:~/git/src/github.com/pico2/wrappers/nodejs# npm run rebuild

> indy-sdk@0.2.1 rebuild /home/ubuntudev/git/src/github.com/pico2/wrappers/nodejs
> node codegen && node-gyp rebuild

make: Entering directory '/home/ubuntudev/git/src/github.com/pico2/wrappers/nodejs/build'
  CXX(target) Release/obj.target/indynodejs/src/indy.o
In file included from ../src/indy_core.h:5:0,
                 from ../src/indy.cc:4:
/home/ubuntudev/git/src/github.com/pico2/wrappers/nodejs/include/indy_types.h:13:23: error: conflicting declaration ‘typedef long long int indy_i64_t’
 typedef long long     indy_i64_t;
                       ^
/home/ubuntudev/git/src/github.com/pico2/wrappers/nodejs/include/indy_types.h:10:23: note: previous declaration as ‘typedef int64_t indy_i64_t’
 typedef int64_t       indy_i64_t;
                       ^
/home/ubuntudev/git/src/github.com/pico2/wrappers/nodejs/include/indy_types.h:14:32: error: conflicting declaration ‘typedef long long unsigned int indy_u64_t’
 typedef unsigned long long     indy_u64_t;
                                ^
/home/ubuntudev/git/src/github.com/pico2/wrappers/nodejs/include/indy_types.h:8:23: note: previous declaration as ‘typedef uint64_t indy_u64_t’
 typedef uint64_t      indy_u64_t;
                       ^
indynodejs.target.mk:97: recipe for target 'Release/obj.target/indynodejs/src/indy.o' failed
```

I wonder how you guys manage to build it, given the name collision? 

Thanks
Stanley

issue_title: Bug in `extraction.py`
issue_body:
When I use `wllvm==1.0.16` and Python 3 and I use the `extract-bc` tool I get

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/dan/dev/whole-program-llvm/venv/bin/extract-bc", line 7, in <module>
    from wllvm.extractor import main
  File "/home/dan/dev/whole-program-llvm/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/wllvm/extractor.py", line 27, in <module>
    from .extraction import extraction
  File "/home/dan/dev/whole-program-llvm/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/wllvm/extraction.py", line 90
    print c
```

If I look at the current sources for this repo I can see the old style Python 2 print statement there. This leads me to ask the following.

* Why didn't TravisCI catch this? It looks like you're testing Python 3 and using `extract-bc` in your tests but the tests passed.

* This bug isn't in https://github.com/travitch/whole-program-llvm despite that being at version `1.0.16`. Is it intentional to be out of sync?

Could the bug be fixed and a new version be pushed out?

issue_title: Ensure `python setup.py test` calls `nosetests`
issue_body:
Integrating `nose` with `setup.py` so that it runs using `python setup.py test` is not straightforward.

The simple approach is to update `setup.py` specifying the following:

```
[...]
test_suite='nose.collector',
[...]
```

Issue is that `nose.collector` doesn't detect nose plugins (e.g coverage). See [here](http://nose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/setuptools_integration.html)

Creating a `NoseTestCommand` in `setup.py` allow to address this. See [here](http://fgimian.github.io/blog/2014/04/27/running-nose-tests-with-plugins-using-the-setuptools-test-command/)

issue_title: While list commands that requires skbuild to run CMake
issue_body:
Instead of back listing commands, as it is currently done in [skbuild/setuptools_wrap.py](https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/blob/fe4b930ea5216fa47456846507851116d28c9895/skbuild/setuptools_wrap.py#L310-L315), commands that requires cmake to be executed should be explicitly listed.

Considering the list of `bdist`, `build` and `install` commands:

```bash
$ python setup.py --help-commands | ack "^\s+(build|bdist|install)"
  build                     build everything needed to install
  build_py                  "build" pure Python modules (copy to build directory)
  build_ext                 build C/C++ extensions (compile/link to build directory)
  build_clib                build C/C++ libraries used by Python extensions
  build_scripts             "build" scripts (copy and fixup #! line)
  install                   install everything from build directory
  install_lib               install all Python modules (extensions and pure Python)
  install_headers           install C/C++ header files
  install_scripts           install scripts (Python or otherwise)
  install_data              install data files
  bdist                     create a built (binary) distribution
  bdist_dumb                create a "dumb" built distribution
  bdist_rpm                 create an RPM distribution
  bdist_wininst             create an executable installer for MS Windows
  bdist_wheel               create a wheel distribution
  install_egg_info          Install an .egg-info directory for the package
  bdist_egg                 create an "egg" distribution
  build_sphinx              Build Sphinx documentation
```

For the one requiring CMake to run, we can trim it to this list for the:

```
  build                     build everything needed to install
  install                   install everything from build directory
  install_lib               install all Python modules (extensions and pure Python)
  bdist                     create a built (binary) distribution
  bdist_dumb                create a "dumb" built distribution
  bdist_rpm                 create an RPM distribution
  bdist_wininst             create an executable installer for MS Windows
  bdist_wheel               create a wheel distribution
  bdist_egg                 create an "egg" distribution
```

For the remaining ones, we will simply delegate to the "regular" setup function ...
```
  build_py                  "build" pure Python modules (copy to build directory)
  build_ext                 build C/C++ extensions (compile/link to build directory)
  build_clib                build C/C++ libraries used by Python extensions
  build_scripts             "build" scripts (copy and fixup #! line)
  install_headers           install C/C++ header files
  install_scripts           install scripts (Python or otherwise)
  install_data              install data files
  install_egg_info          Install an .egg-info directory for the package
  build_sphinx              Build Sphinx documentation
```

issue_title: Inclusion of dust UTXOs in account balance
issue_body:
Currently, core includes dust UTXOs in your account balance. When verifying that PREORDER, REGISTER, UPDATE, TRANSFER will all succeed, core will include the dust UTXOs in that verification. However, these aren't spendable.

To fix this, the price check in `safety.py`, should only include non-dust UTXOs in its calculation. This will probably require adding a parameter to the balance calculation. It will also need a more verbose error response than currently supported.

                                                     length)

-    name_value = Name.NameValue('Test Key')
-    name_value = Name.NameValue('TestKey')
   name_type = Name.NameType(NameType.UNINTERPRETED_TEXT_STRING)

issue_title: Brackets enclosing values (i. e. {{Institutional Author}}) are removed
issue_body:
The parser does a little bit too much cleanup. Entire values enclosed in brackets are stripped of the enclosing brackets. There are numerous instances where bracketing entire values are appropriate, for example with institutional authors. An example:

```
@book{SillyWalks1970,
 author = {{Ministry of Silly Walks}},
 location = {London},
 publisher = {We Publish Anything},
 title = {Handbook of Silly Walks},
 year = {1970}
}
```

The above bibtex entry yields (linebreaks added for readability):

```
[{u'publisher': u'We Publish Anything',
 u'title': u'Handbook of Silly Walks',
 u'author': u'Ministry of Silly Walks',
 'ID': 'SillyWalks1970',
 u'location': u'London',
 u'year': u'1970',
 'ENTRYTYPE': u'book'}]
```

Value of `author` should be `u'{Ministry of Silly Walks}'`, with brackets left untouched.

issue_title: Entries with only one field not correctly parsed
issue_body:
An entry with only one field is not correctly parsed if the field doesn't end with a comma. So

> @misc{noteName,
> note = "A note with some text"
>}

is not correctly parsed, while

> @misc{noteName,
> note = "A note with some text"**,**
>}

is working.

Tested on bibtexparser 0.6.2

issue_title: Make entities JSON Serializable
issue_body:
in #321 @ehelms discovered that nailgun entities are not JSONSerializable.

I made a simple test.

```python
In [1]: from nailgun.entities import Organization
In [2]: org = Organization()
In [3]: import json
In [4]: json.dumps(org)
/usr/lib64/python2.7/json/encoder.pyc in default(self, o)
    182 
    183         """
--> 184         raise TypeError(repr(o) + " is not JSON serializable")
    185 
    186     def encode(self, o):

TypeError: Organization(nailgun.config.ServerConfig(....)) is not JSON serializable
```

That happens because `__repr__` of entities are not returning values that default JSON Decoder is able to parse, also there is no `to_json` method and no recursive traversing of nested entities.

We can develop all of this builtin, but looks like a lot of work, so:

I am suggesting to use Marshmallow[1] as it is a known and stable existing library to give  Schema Validation and Serialization to nailgun.

[1] https://marshmallow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

issue_title: READ_CONTACTS abusively reported for openInputStream
issue_body:
Hi,
Androguard is reporting use of READ_CONTACTS whenever there is this in the code:

```java
localInputStream =
this.context.getContentResolver().openInputStream(paramUri);
```

This is abusive, because depending on paramUri, the code above can be used to load an image or plenty of other things. Contacts are really read only if you access ContactsContract.xxxx.

In Androguard's code, you should probably re-visit api_permissions.py and modify/remove:

```python
"READ_CONTACTS" : {
	"Landroid/app/ContextImpl$ApplicationContentResolver;" : [
		("F", "openFileDescriptor", "(Landroid/net/Uri; Ljava/lang/String;)"),
		("F", "openInputStream", "(Landroid/net/Uri;)"),
		("F", "openOutputStream", "(Landroid/net/Uri;)"),
		("F", "query", "(Landroid/net/Uri; [L[Ljava/lang/Strin; Ljava/lang/String; [L[Ljava/lang/Strin; Ljava/lang/String;)"),
	],
```

issue_title: releases.json contains undownloadable release
issue_body:
# Issue Title

The releases.json file on master contains a 2.0.8 release which can't be downloaded by dotnet-install.ps1.

# General

We have dozens of applications and they could all be on different versions of .NET Core. We are writing automation to download the version of the .NET Core runtime at deploy time. We are using each project's TargetFramework property (e.g. netcoreapp1.0, netcoreapp2.0, etc.) to download the latest patch version of each target framework. We use the releases.json file to find the latest patch version.

Unfortunately, the latest version of 2.0 (according to releases.json) is 2.0.8. When we use dotnet-install.ps1 to download 2.0.8, we get a 404 error.

Here's the PowerShell code we wrote to do the download and install [1]. It would be nice if the releases.json file didn't contain releases that couldn't be downloaded or had some kind of flag to filter them out.

[1]
```
[CmdletBinding()]
param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory=$true)]
    [string]
    # The name of the package in ProGet.
    $TargetFramework,

    [string]
    # The directory where the .NET Core runtime should be installed. Defaults to `$env:ProgramFiles`.
    $InstallRoot = $env:ProgramFiles
)

#Requires -Version 4
Set-StrictMode -Version 'Latest'

$targetFramework -match '^netcoreapp(.*)$'

$majorMinor = '{0}.*' -f $Matches[1]

$runtimeVersion = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dotnet/core/master/release-notes/releases.json' |
                        ForEach-Object { $_ } |
                        Where-Object { $_ | Get-Member 'version-runtime' } |
                        Where-Object { 
                            $_.'version-runtime' -like $majorMinor 
                        } |
                        Where-Object { 
                            [version]$version = $null ; 
                            return [Version]::TryParse($_.'version-runtime', [ref]$version)
                        } |
                        Sort-Object -Property { [version]($_.'version-runtime') } -Descending |
                        Select-Object -First 1 |
                        Select-Object -ExpandProperty 'version-runtime'

$installDotNetPath = Join-Path -Path $PSScriptRoot -ChildPath 'bin\dotnet-install.ps1' -Resolve

$installDir = Join-Path -Path $InstallRoot -ChildPath ('dotnet-{0}' -f $runtimeVersion)

& $installDotNetPath -Version $runtimeVersion -SharedRuntime -Installdir $installDir
```

issue_title: Default middleware priority 610 is too close to downloader for compressed responses.
issue_body:
The readme suggests to use these settings:

    DOWNLOADER_MIDDLEWARES = {
        # ...
        'rotating_proxies.middlewares.RotatingProxyMiddleware': 610,
        'rotating_proxies.middlewares.BanDetectionMiddleware': 620,
        # ...
    }

A problem can arise here is that `BanDetectonMiddleware` runs before base downloader middleware `scrapy.downloadermiddlewares.httpcompression.HttpCompressionMiddleware` (it's on 590), so if you were to try to access `response.body` or `response.body_as_unicode()` in your ban policy you'd get an error on compressed responses:

    raise NotSupported("Response content isn't text")

So to avoid this issue the recommended location for rotating_proxies middlewares should be < 590, and probably above `RetryMiddleware` 550.

issue_title: Disable automatic -0001 suffixes in cert storage
issue_body:
# Scenario

I start with generating a single cert for "example.com, www.example.com, test.example.com", this is stored in `/etc/letsencrypt/archive/example.com`

Later I generate a new cert with "example.com, www.example.com"
This will be stored in `/etc/letsencrypt/archive/example.com-0001`

At this stage it is unclear whether the user intended to remove `test.example.com` or to generate a new cert.

# Suggestion

With this issue I suggest removal of the automatic suffix generation.

The risks are that one might not notice the suffix and assume that the new certs are in the original location.
The location of the generated certs should be known before running the client.
I'm not suggesting silently replacing the cert in the original location.

The algorithm that triggers the use of prefix should still be in use but once triggered I suggest the following approach.

When triggered an error will occur.
The error message will instruct that one of the following approaches is required.

1. Indicate that you want to modify the domains in the current cert. This feature is suggested in #2071 
2. Choose a new alias for the storage using an option like `--alias`

With option 2 the use of `--alias example2` would store the certs in `/etc/letsencrypt/archive/example2`

These two options can work independently and combined.

# Examples

If your intention was to remove `test.example.com` then you use 1

If you intention was to generate another different cert then use 2

If you want to further modify the domain list for the cert in the new location you must use both 1 and 2.

issue_title: consumer segmentation fault upon subscribe a second time
issue_body:
Hi,

I don't know much about kafka, so it's very possible I'm using it incorrectly, in which case apologies in advance.

I have a Consumer object. I subscribed it to a kafka topic and was able to receive messages fine with it. I'm having the consumer stop consuming and closing upon reaching end of partition. However, if I were to then use the same consumer to subscribe again to the same topic, I get a segmentation fault.

**Versions**
python 2.7.5, confluent_kafka 0.9.4, librdkafka 0.9.4

```
[confluent-kafka] > pip list
DEPRECATION: The default format will switch to columns in the future. You can use --format=(legacy|columns) (or define a format=(legacy|columns) in your pip.conf under the [list] section) to disable this warning.
certifi (2017.4.17)
chardet (3.0.4)
confluent-kafka (0.9.4)
dnspython (1.12.0)
funcsigs (1.0.2)
idna (2.5)
mock (2.0.0)
pbr (3.0.1)
pip (9.0.1)
py (1.4.34)
pytest (3.0.1)
pytest-runner (2.9)
requests (2.17.3)
setuptools (36.0.1)
six (1.10.0)
urllib3 (1.21.1)
wheel (0.30.0a0)
```

```
[confluent-kafka] > rpm -qa | grep librdkafka
librdkafka-0.9.4-1.el7.x86_64
librdkafka-devel-0.9.4-1.el7.x86_64
```

**Steps To Reproduce**

1. start up kafka
(I followed this: https://github.com/confluentinc/cp-docker-images/wiki/Getting-Started)
```
docker run -d --net=host --name=zookeeper -e ZOOKEEPER_CLIENT_PORT=32181 -e ZOOKEEPER_TICK_TIME=2000 confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:3.0.0
docker run -d --net=host --name=kafka -e KAFKA_ZOOKEEPER_CONNECT=localhost:32181 -e KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://localhost:29092 confluentinc/cp-kafka:3.0.0
docker run --net=host --rm confluentinc/cp-kafka:3.0.0 kafka-topics --create --topic test.topic --partitions 1 --replication-factor 1 --if-not-exists --zookeeper localhost:32181
```
(I also tested against our production kafka and saw the same segfault)

2. run following code:
```python
from confluent_kafka import Producer, Consumer, KafkaError, KafkaException

KAFKA_SERVER = 'localhost:29092'
KAFKA_TOPIC = 'test.topic'
CONSUMER_GROUP = 'test-group'

CONSUMER_CONFIG = {'bootstrap.servers': KAFKA_SERVER,
                   'enable.auto.commit': False,
                   'group.id': CONSUMER_GROUP,
                   'default.topic.config': {'auto.offset.reset': 'earliest'}}


def sendKafkaMessage(topic, message):
    producer = Producer({'bootstrap.servers': KAFKA_SERVER})
    producer.produce(topic, message)
    producer.flush()


def getKafkaMessages(consumer):
    messages = []

    consumer.subscribe([KAFKA_TOPIC])
    try:
        while True:
            message = consumer.poll(timeout=1.0)
            if message is None:
                continue
            error = message.error()
            if error:
                if error.code() == KafkaError._PARTITION_EOF:
                    break
                else:
                    raise KafkaException(error)
            else:
                messages.append(message.value())
                consumer.commit(async=False)
    finally:
        consumer.close()

    return messages


if __name__ == '__main__':
    consumer = Consumer(**CONSUMER_CONFIG)

    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message1')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message2')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message3')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message4')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message5')
    print getKafkaMessages(consumer)

    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message6')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message7')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message8')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message9')
    sendKafkaMessage(KAFKA_TOPIC, 'message10')
    print getKafkaMessages(consumer)
```

This will segfault during the second `getKafkaMessages` call, specifically when `consumer.subscribe` runs again. I'm expecting that the consumer would be able to subscribe again and pick up where it left off via consumer group and commit.

Is this a bug or user error?

Thanks for your time!
Jack

issue_title: End Explicit Support for Python 3.3
issue_body:
Python 3.3 has been EoL'ed since September, and more and more major packages are dropping support, making maintenance more difficult. Pytest, for example, [dropped support in version 3.3.0](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/blob/master/CHANGELOG.rst#pytest-330-2017-11-23).

I think that unless there is a really good reason, we should stop explicitly supporting Python 3.3 going forward.

issue_title: Weird extra tag 'v'
issue_body: If you check the [older releases](https://github.com/Calysto/metakernel/releases?after=v0.11.5), there's a tag named `v`. Based on the other tags and the commit, I think this should actually be `v0.10.4`, and `v` should be deleted.

issue_title: Fails to parse the time of day with full stops as seperator
issue_body:
Full stops are commonly used as sepators between hours, minutes and seconds in my locale. This trips up the dateutil parser, as shown by these examples:

`dateutil.parser.parse('17.05.2014 11:15', dayfirst=True)` works without a hitch.
`dateutil.parser.parse('17.05.2014 11.15', dayfirst=True)` raises a `ValueError: Unknown string format`

Tried in dateutil version 2.5.0 and 2.6.1

issue_title: datetime.timedelta cannot be added to relativedelta
issue_body:
```python
from dateutil import relativedelta
import datetime

relativedelta.relativedelta(years=1) + datetime.timedelta(days=1)
```

Results in a `TypeError: unsupported type for add operation`, despite both being time offsets.

However, it is perfectly possible to add a `relativedelta` to another `relativedelta`:

```python

relativedelta.relativedelta(years=1) + relativedelta.relativedelta(years=1)
# relativedelta(years=+2)
```

I'm happy to submit a PR if there is interest (beyond myself) in this.

issue_title: Release 2.6.0
issue_body:
The current version on github has all the changes currently scheduled for the 2.6.0 release. Like 2.5.0, this another big one - mostly because of the time it took to get the ambiguous datetime stuff right. Here is the new NEWS file (which should ping those who have made contributions):

- Added PEP-495-compatible methods to address ambiguous and imaginary dates in
  time zones in a backwards-compatible way. Ambiguous dates and times can now
  be safely represented by all dateutil time zones. Many thanks to Alexander
  Belopolski (@abalkin) and Tim Peters @tim-one for their inputs on how to
  address this. Original issues reported by Yupeng and @zed (lP: 1390262,
  gh issues #57, #112, #249, #284, #286, prs #127, #225, #248, #264, #302).
- Added new methods for working with ambiguous and imaginary dates to the tz
  module. datetime_ambiguous() determines if a datetime is ambiguous for a given
  zone and datetime_exists() determines if a datetime exists in a given zone.
  This works for all fold-aware datetimes, not just those provided by dateutil.
  (gh issue #253, gh pr #302)
- Fixed an issue where dst() in Portugal in 1996 was returning the wrong value
  in tz.tzfile objects. Reported by @abalkin (gh issue #128, pr #225)
- Fixed an issue where zoneinfo.ZoneInfoFile errors were not being properly
  deep-copied. (gh issue #226, pr #225)
- Refactored tzwin and tzrange as a subclass of a common class, tzrangebase, as
  there was substantial overlapping functionality. As part of this change,
  tzrange and tzstr now expose a transitions() function, which returns the
  DST on and off transitions for a given year. (gh issue #260, pr #302)
- Deprecated zoneinfo.gettz() due to confusion with tz.gettz(), in favor of
  get() method of zoneinfo.ZoneInfoFile objects. (gh issue #11, pr #310)
- For non-character, non-stream arguments, parser.parse now raises TypeError
  instead of AttributeError. (gh issues #171, #269, pr #247)
- Fixed an issue where tzfile objects were not properly handling dst() and
  tzname() when attached to datetime.time objects. Reported by @ovacephaloid.
  (gh issue #292, pr #309)
- /usr/share/lib/zoneinfo was added to TZPATHS for compatibility with Solaris
  systems. Reported by @dhduvall (gh issue #276, pr #307)
- tzoffset and tzrange objects now accept either a number of seconds or a
  datetime.timedelta() object wherever previously only a number of seconds was
  allowed. (gh pr #264, #277)
- datetime.timedelta objects can now be added to relativedelta objects. Reported
  and added by Alec Nikolas Reiter (@justanr) (gh issue #282, pr #283
- Refactored relativedelta.weekday and rrule.weekday into a common base class
  to reduce code duplication. (gh issue #140, pr #311)
- An issue where the WKST parameter was improperly rendering in str(rrule) was
  reported and fixed by Daniel LePage (@dplepage). (gh issue #262, pr #263)
- A replace() method has been added to rrule objects by @jendas1, which creates
  new rrule with modified attributes, analogous to datetime.replace (gh pr #167)
- Made some significant performance improvements to rrule objects in Python 2.x
  (gh pr #245)
- All classes defining equality functions now return NotImplemented when
  compared to unsupported classes, rather than raising TypeError, to allow other
  classes to provide fallback support. (gh pr #236)
- Several classes have been marked as explicitly unhashable to maintain
  identical behavior between Python 2 and 3. Submitted by Roy Williams 
  (@rowillia) (gh pr #296)
- Trailing whitespace in easter.py has been removed. Submitted by @OmgImAlexis
  (gh pr #299)
- Windows-only batch files in build scripts had line endings switched to CRLF.
  (gh pr #237)
- @adamchainz updated the documentation links to reflect that the canonical
  location for readthedocs links is now at .io, not .org. (gh pr #272)
- Made some changes to the CI and codecov to test against newer versions of
  Python and pypy, and to adjust the code coverage requirements. For the moment,
  full pypy3 compatibility is not supported until a new release is available,
  due to upstream bugs in the old version affecting PEP-495 support.
  (gh prs #265, #266, #304, #308)
- The full PGP signing key fingerprint was added to the README.md in favor of
  the previously used long-id. Reported by @valholl (gh issue #287, pr #304)
- Updated zoneinfo to 2016i. (gh issue #298, gh pr #306)

If anyone has any objections or last-minute corrections, please let me know - where the name on github looks like a real name, or where I've spoken to people publicly using real names, I've included real names along with github handles, if you would like your real name removed or changed, or your github handle removed or changed, please let me know and I'll remove it). I'll note that I haven't been too concerned with the documentation yet, but that should be coming relatively soon. I think that there will also be some additional behind-the-scenes improvements to the way `fromutc()` is handled for most of the `tz` classes provided here, but for now they should all work.

(Ping @jarondl)

issue_title: Add support for Facebook instant articles
Allow widgets to be embedded in articles
issue_body:
We want to add a new article embed type that allows the user to embed a widget in an article (just like an image, video, etc).

Embeds are currently defined here: https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/tree/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/vendor/dispatch-editor/embeds

and are loaded by the editor here: https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/components/ArticleEditor/ArticleContentEditor.js#L14-L20

I'm proposing we create a new `WidgetEmbed` that allows the user to select a widget and fill out the fields, much like how the widget editor currently works. 

You should be able to use [WidgetSelectInput](https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/components/inputs/WidgetSelectInput.js) to give uses the ability to choose the widget. Once a widget is chosen, the fields should be rendered below the input, much like the [current zone editor](https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/components/ZoneEditor/index.js#L38-L77).

On the backend, we should define a default zone called "Embed" in `dispatch/theme` to represent an article embed. This allows users to define which of their widgets are compatible with article embeds.

To save widget embed data, you'll need to define a new embed controller to handle widgets. I suggest using [CodeController](https://github.com/ubyssey/dispatch/blob/develop/dispatch/static/manager/src/js/components/ZoneEditor/index.js#L38-L77) as an example. The `render` method should return the result of rendering the widget with the data provided in the editor. I believe you can accomplish this by saving the widget ID and data as JSON.

As always, feel free to reach out on Slack if you have any questions!
